task: I’m trying to explain to a friend why a part can look fine at first but still cause trouble later in the process, and I want to keep it simple and accurate. Can you help me phrase that in plain English without making it sound too technical?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — you could say:

“Even if a part looks okay at first, it can still cause problems later because some issues don’t show up until it’s used or tested further.”

If you want, I can also give you:
- a more casual version,
- a more professional version, or
- a one-sentence version for texting.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s basically it. But make it a little tighter.

Maybe:
“Sometimes a part looks fine at first, but a small problem doesn’t show up until later when it’s used, tested, or moved through the next step.”

That’s plain enough. If you want, give me a version that sounds more like something I’d say on the floor, not so polished.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a more natural, “on the floor” version:

“Sometimes a part looks good at first, but there’s a small issue that doesn’t show up until later when it gets used or checked again.”

If you want, I can make it even more casual or more like shop-talk.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Sounds like something I’d actually say. Clean and plain.
